prompt:
A cylindrical 5 cm ceramic air stone with a slightly rough, porous surface lies on a damp work surface next to a short length of flexible airline tubing. One end of the air stone has a small plastic or metal barb where the tubing can attach, clearly showing how it integrates into an air pump system. Behind it, a partially visible hydroponic reservoir or bucket with gently bubbling water suggests how the stone diffuses air into the nutrient solution when in use.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk hydroponics area with soft, even lighting, healthy plants in the background, and neatly routed tubing along the edges of a grow tub. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with light stylization, no logos or text on the stone, and a composition that centers the air stone and tubing connection while the rest of the hydroponic equipment and greenery remains softly blurred, emphasizing its role as a bubble diffuser in reservoirs.
